I think living in the US makes me way happier, indeed. First, I love doing science. In, HK, I don't think I would get as much opportunity as here in SF Bay Area. As working in a university, I only needed to contribute about 10% (150 USD) per month towards the health insurance for me and my wife while the university pays the rest.

For the environment, I think there is way less pollution in here. My home is close to a state park so I got tons of fresh air.

For entertainment, hmmm, I don't need to go out a lot so I have no problems living in here at all. Sometimes friends come to my place for hot pot dinner then play games til late night. Of course, these all base on the fact that I have my own place to have fun with my friends.

Moreover, yup there is discrimination among Chinese people or from other races. In SF Bay Area, a single "Fuck off I'm a fucking homeowner." would be more than enough to shut most of the people up.

Is HK good to live in? Hell yea if you have money. However, I still prefer to live in the US, in my tiny own castle without the fear of the end of the leasehold period.
